threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: When you still call it 'the tube' instead of 'the Overground' and look confused when people tell you to catch a train from Hackney Central.
Caught a night bus from Brixton to Clapham and a bloke claimed he invented a drink that cures hangovers. The bus driver just nodded, clearly heard it all before.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a pigeon stealing a kebab from a tourist's hand and all the locals just laughed. That’s Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you grab a beer from a Späti, sit on the curb, and watch a spontaneous DJ set emerge out of nowhere, all by sunset.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their go-to taco truck; if they don’t know it’s El Farolito, they’re just passing through.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Twice last week, the 49 bus rolled by with its lights off—driver on break while the rest of us crammed into the next one. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think they can only eat paella by the beach. The best seafood is hidden in Gràcia’s little tavernas, where locals go for fresh catch.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll notice them fumbling with the metro map while everyone else is glued to their phones, clearly still relying on paper for directions.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when someone asks if you want your toast 'sourdough or rye' at the café. It’s a dead giveaway.
Caught the N137 last night; a guy in a leopard print coat was debating life choices with a potted plant. The driver didn’t bat an eye.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted a guy brewing beer in his living room while chatting with a neighbor about the latest BVG delays. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab stand that doubles as an art gallery, where the chef also hosts poetry nights. It's a true local gem.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local if they don't flinch at the Muni delays but get mad if you talk trash about the burritos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just watched a dog stroll down the Civic Center track while buses honked in frustration. Only in San Francisco do we stop for furry VIPs.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every bar in Gràcia serves tapas, but many locals head straight for the patates braves at the tiny spot near the square. It's a game changer.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their constant hesitation at the metro ticket machines, still too shy to ask for help.
